target.name: AIR
target.founder: Rani Plaut, Chen Rosen, Netanel Goldberg

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $23M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
AIR, a Series A transportation company founded by Rani Plaut, Chen Rosen, and Netanel Goldberg, operates within a private-heavy frontier-tech sector. The comparable universe includes eight private firms across transportation—notably Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Pipedream, and FleetZero—with no public peers available for direct benchmarking.

Valuation triangulation relies on a single applicable methodology: stage-progression analysis. Using AIR's $23M total capital raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-subsequent-round step-ups yields an implied valuation of $46M. A peer-median approach could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the transportation cohort; only three firms share the sector, and none met the threshold of three datapoints within relevant valuation tiers. Broader cross-sector medians were excluded to avoid introducing misleading anchors.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $39M to $53M, with a median point of $46M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single method rather than triangulated consensus across approaches. Key uncertainties include the true post-Series A step-up multiplier for this specific sub-sector, the representativeness of disclosed transportation comparables, and AIR's revenue, unit economics, or capital efficiency relative to the peer set—none of which are provided in available data.
